    Match review

    Barcelona 3-0 Mallorca: Yamal stunner helps LaLiga leaders move four points clear

    About the match

      Barcelona faces Mallorca at Spotify Camp Nou on Sat, Feb 7, 2026, 15:15 UTC. This match is part of the LaLiga. You can check the recent head-to-head encounters, as well as full H2H record on this page to see how Barcelona and Mallorca have fared against each other in the past. On FotMob, you can follow the Barcelona vs Mallorca live score with a full set of match features, including:

    • Live updates: Every goal, card, substitution and key moment instantly delivered on FotMob.

    • Real-time extensive stats powered by Opta: Possession, shots, corners, big chances created, xG, momentum, and shot maps.

    • The lineups are:
      Barcelona (4-3-3): Joan Garcia - Jules Koundé, Pau Cubarsi, Eric Garcia, Alejandro Balde - Fermin Lopez, Marc Casado, Dani Olmo - Lamine Yamal, Robert Lewandowski, Marcus Rashford.
      Mallorca (4-2-3-1): Leo Román - Pablo Maffeo, David Lopez, Martin Valjent, Johan Mojica - Omar Mascarell, Samu - Mateu Morey, Pablo Torre, Jan Virgili - Vedat Muriqi.

    • Unavailable players for Barcelona: Andreas Christensen (injury), Gavi (injury), Pedri (injury), Raphinha (injury). Unavailable players for Mallorca: Takuma Asano (injury).

    • Team form & Head-to-head history: Compare recent results and see how Barcelona and Mallorca have performed against each other. The current head to head record for the teams are Barcelona 15 win(s), Mallorca 0 win(s), and 2 draw(s).

    • TV and streaming info: Find out where to watch the match.

    • Live standings: Follow league tables and tournament info in real time.

    • Live odds & insights: Track match favorites and before, during and post match.

    • Commentary & ticker: Rich text commentary for major matches to follow the action even if you can't watch.

    Insights

    • Lamine Yamal has been involved in 33 goals in his last 37 appearances

    • Barcelona scored 16 goals in their last five matches

    • Barcelona have won 11 of their last 12 LaLiga matches

    • Barcelona conceded just four goals in seven home matches since November

    • Mallorca have won just one of their last 15 away matches

    • Mallorca sit 14th but rank 17th in the xG table, overperforming by three positions

    • Mallorca haven't kept a clean sheet in eight matches

    • Vedat Muriqi scored a hat-trick in Mallorca's 3-2 win over Athletic Bilbao last month

    Match Review

    Barcelona 3-0 Mallorca: Yamal stunner helps LaLiga leaders move four points clear
    Barcelona continued their quest for LaLiga glory in style with a victory over Mallorca, with Lamine Yamal scoring the pick of the goals.
    Opta

    Insights

    • Lamine Yamal has been involved in 33 goals in his last 37 appearances
    • Barcelona scored 16 goals in their last five matches
    • Barcelona have won 11 of their last 12 LaLiga matches
    • Barcelona conceded just four goals in seven home matches since November
    • Mallorca have won just one of their last 15 away matches
    • Mallorca sit 14th but rank 17th in the xG table, overperforming by three positions
